title: Find First Duplicate in an Array (Edge Case Handling)

statement.markdown: Implement a function to find the first duplicate in an array of integers. If no duplicates are found, return -1. The function must handle edge cases where the array is empty or has a single element.
statement.outputFormat: The index of the first duplicate (or -1 if no duplicates are found)
statement.notes: Note that the array may contain negative numbers.

hints.text: Use a HashSet to keep track of seen elements

editorial.title: Finding First Duplicate in an Array
editorial.intuition: Hashing can help in finding duplicates efficiently
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: check every element against the rest of the array
editorial.betterSolution: Use a HashSet to keep track of seen elements
editorial.optimalSolution: Iterate through the array, using a HashSet to keep track of seen elements, and return the index of the first duplicate
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Hashing ensures O(1) lookup time, making it efficient
editorial.dryRun: Iterate through the array, using a HashSet to keep track of seen elements, and return the index of the first duplicate
commonMistakes: Not using a HashSet for efficient lookup

functionSignature.java: public int findFirstDuplicate(int A[], int n);
